issue_title: Let creators publish a saved template to the gallery
issue_body:
**Phase:** Expand — adding the next capability

Add the next vertical slice for power users: after saving a show template, a creator can turn it into a shareable gallery listing with a preview, name, short description, included style elements, and a free or paid label. Other users should be able to discover that listing in the creator template gallery and start a new episode from it, carrying the layout and style forward.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
From a saved template, the running UI offers a clear publish/share action. Completing it creates a visible gallery listing with the template preview, creator-facing description, and free/paid label. Selecting that listing starts a new episode setup/workspace with the published layout and style already applied. Existing verification still passes with `node scripts/run-tests.mjs`.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The core product already supports the full podcast production path and has been polished through import and preset usability. The strongest remaining expansion from the vision is serving power users who want reusable and eventually monetizable custom podcast layouts; publishing saved templates to a gallery is the smallest complete step toward that without jumping into payment infrastructure._
